mnemonics: FJDPIE - Full Joint Distributions Provide Inference Evidence (Think of it as the 'Fudge Pie' you use to reason about the world: Full Joint, Distribution, Probability, Inference, Evidence)
keyPoints: A full joint distribution is a complete probability table assigning a probability to every possible combination of values for the random variables in the domain.
It serves as a complete knowledge base from which any desired probability can be answered through the process of marginalization (summing out) and conditioning.
This method, while conceptually straightforward and powerful, becomes computationally intractable for large numbers of variables due to the exponential growth of the table size.
commonMistakes: Confusing the order of variables when summing out (marginalizing) and conditioning, which can lead to incorrect probability calculations.
Forgetting to normalize the probabilities after conditioning on evidence, resulting in values that do not sum to 1.
